verses.t: Sha’ul and Timothy, servants of OSwhy Mashiach, to all the set-apart ones in Mashiach OSwhy who are in Philippi, with the overseers and attendants:
Chen to you and shalom from Aluahim our Father and the Master OSwhy Mashiach.
I thank my Aluahim in all my remembrance of you.
In all my prayers for all of you, I always pray with joy,
for your fellowship in the Besorah from the first day until now,
being persuaded of this, that He who has begun a good work in you shall perfect it until the day of OSwhy Mashiach.
It is right for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart, all of you being sharers of the chen with me, both in my chains and in the defence and confirmation of the Besorah.
For Aluahim is my witness, how I long for you all with the affection of OSwhy Mashiach.
And this I pray, that your love might extend more and more in knowledge and all discernment,
for you to examine the matters that differ, in order to be sincere and not stumbling, until the day of Mashiach,
being filled with the fruit of righteousness, through OSwhy Mashiach, to the esteem and praise of Aluahim.
And I wish you to know, brothers, that what has befallen me has turned out for the advancement of the Besorah,
so that it has become known to all the palace guard and to all the rest, that my chains are in Mashiach;
and most of the brothers, trusting in the Master because of my chains, are much more bold to fearlessly speak the word.
Some indeed proclaim Mashiach even from envy and strife, but some also out of pleasure –
the former announce Mashiach from selfish ambition, not sincerely, thinking to add distress to my chains,
but the latter out of love, knowing that I am appointed for the defence of the Besorah.
What then? Only that in every way, whether in pretence or in truth, Mashiach is announced. And in this I rejoice, in fact, I shall rejoice.
For I know that this shall turn out for my deliverance through your prayer and the supply of the Ruach of OSwhy Mashiach,

according to my intense longing and anticipation that I shall not be ashamed at all, but that with all boldness, as always, so now also Mashiach shall be made great in my body, whether by life or by death.
For to me, to live is Mashiach and to die is gain.
And if to live in the flesh is to me a fruit of work, then what shall I choose? I do not know.
For I am pressed down by the two, having a desire to depart and be with Mashiach, which is much better,
but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for your sake.
And being persuaded of this, I know that I shall stay and continue with you all, for your progress and joy of belief,
so that your boasting might overflow in OSwhy Mashiach on account of me, through my coming to you again.

Only, behave yourselves worthily of the Besorah of Mashiach, in order that whether I come and see you or am absent, I hear about you, that you stand fast in one ruach, with one being, striving together for the belief of the Besorah,
without being frightened in any way by those who oppose, which to them truly is a proof of destruction, but to you of deliverance and that from Aluahim.
Because to you it has been given as a chen, on behalf of Mashiach, not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake,
having the same struggle which you saw in me and now hear to be in me.
If, then, there is any encouragement in Mashiach, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of Ruach, if any affection and compassion,
make my joy complete by being of the same mind, having the same love, one in being and of purpose,
doing none at all through selfishness or self-conceit, but in humility consider others better than yourselves.
Each one should look out not only for his own interests, but also for the interests of others.
For, let this mind be in you which was also in Mashiach OSwhy,
who, being in the form of Aluahim, did not regard equality with Aluahim a matter to be grasped,
but emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant and came to be in the likeness of men.
And having been found in fashion as a man, He humbled Himself and became obedient unto death, death even of a stake.
Aluahim, therefore, has highly exalted Him and given Him the Name which is above every name,
that at the Name of OSwhy every knee should bow, of those in shamayim and of those on earth and of those under the earth,
and every tongue should confess that OSwhy Mashiach is Master, to the esteem of Aluahim the Father.
So that, my beloved , as you always obeyed – not only in my presence, but now much rather in my absence – work out your own deliverance with fear and trembling,
for it is Aluahim who is working in you both to desire and to work for His good pleasure.
Do all matters without grumblings and disputings,
in order that you be blameless and faultless, children of Aluahim without blemish in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you shine as lights in the world,
holding on to the Word of life, for a boast to me in the day of Mashiach, that I have not run in vain or laboured in vain.
In fact, even if I am being poured out as a drink offering on the offering and service of your belief, I am glad and rejoice with you all.
So you too should be glad and rejoice with me.
But I trust in the Master OSwhy to send Timothy to you shortly, so that I too am encouraged by news from you.
For I have no one Alse of the same mind, who takes a genuine interest in your welfare.
For all seek their own interests, not those of Mashiach OSwhy.
But you know he has proven himself, that as a son with his father he served with me for the Besorah.
So I expect to send him as soon as I see how it goes with me,
and I trust in the Master that I myself shall also come shortly.
But I thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditos, my brother, fellow worker and fellow soldier and your emissary and servant to my need,
since he was longing for you all and being troubled because you had heard that he was sick,
For indeed he was sick, near to death, but Aluahim had compassion on him and not only on him but on me as well, lest I should have sadness upon sadness.
Therefore I sent him more eagerly, so that on seeing him again you might rejoice and I be less sad.
Receive him therefore in the Master with all joy and hold such as he in esteem,
because for the work of Mashiach he was near death, risking his life, to fill up what was lacking in your service toward me.
For the rest, my brothers, rejoice in HWHY. To write the same matters to you is truly no trouble to me and for you it is safe.
Look out for dogs, look out for the evil workers, look out for the mutilation!
For we are the circumcision, who are serving Aluahim in the Ruach and boasting in Mashiach OSwhy and do not trust in the flesh,
though I too might have trust in the flesh. If anyone Alse thinks to trust in the flesh, I more –
circumcised the eighth day, of the race of Yasharal, of the tribe of Binyamin, a Heḇrew of Heḇrews, according to Torah a Pharisee,
according to ardour, persecuting the qahal; according to righteousness that is in the law, having become blameless.
But what might have been a gain to me, I have counted as loss, because of Mashiach.

What is more, I even count all to be loss because of the excellence of the knowledge of Mashiach OSwhy my Master, for whom I have suffered the loss of all and count them as refuse, in order to gain Mashiach,
and be found in Him, not having my own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through belief in Mashiach, the righteousness which is from Aluahim on the basis of belief,
to know Him and the power of His resurrection and the fellowship of His sufferings, being conformed to His death,
if somehow I might attain to the resurrection from the dead.
Not that I have already received, or already been perfected, but I press on, to lay hold of that for which Mashiach OSwhy has also laid hold of me.
Brothers, I do not count myself to have laid hold of it yet, but only this: forgetting what is behind and reaching out for what lies ahead,
I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of Aluahim in Mashiach OSwhy.
As many, then, as are perfect, should have this mind. And if you think differently in any respect, Aluahim shall also reveal this to you.
But to what we have already attained – walk by the same rule, be of the same mind.
Become joint imitators of me, brothers and observe those who so walk, as you have us for a pattern.
For many – of whom I have often told you and now say to you even weeping – walk asenemies of the stake of Mashiach.
Their end is destruction, their mighty one is their stomach and their esteem is in their shame – they mind the earthly.
For our citizenship is in the shamayim, from which we also eagerly wait for the Saviour, the Master OSwhy Mashiach,
who shall change our lowly body, to be conformed to His esteemed body, according to the working by which He is able even to bring all under His control.
So then, my brothers, beloved and longed-for, my joy and crown, stand firm in the Master, beloved .
I appeal to Euodia and I appeal to Suntuche to be of the same mind in the Master.
And I also ask you, true companion, help these women who laboured with me in the Besorah, with Qlemes also and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the Book of life.
Rejoice in HWHY always, again I say, rejoice!
Let your gentleness be known to all men. The Master is near.
Do not worry at all, but in every matter, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to Aluahim. esteem forever and ever. Amĕn.
And the shalom of Aluahim, which
For the rest, brothers, whatever is most of all those of Caesar’s household. true, whatever is noble, whatever is
And what you have learned and received and heard and saw in me, practise these and the Aluahim of shalom shall be with you.
And I rejoiced in HWHY greatly that now at last your concern for me has revived again – though you were concerned, but had no chance.
Not that I speak concerning need, for I have learned to be content in whatever state I am.
I know what it is to be humbled and I know what it is to have in excess. In any and every situation I have learned both to be filled and to be hungry, both to have in excess and to be in need.
I have strength to do all, through Mashiach who empowers me.
Yet you did well in sharing in my pressure .
And you know too, Philippians, that in the beginning of the Besorah, when I went out from Makedonia, no qahal shared with me concerning giving and receiving, except you only,
because, even in Thessalonike you sent to my need, once and twice.
Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the fruit that is multiplying to your account.
Indeed I have all and more than enough. I have been filled, having received from Epaphroditos what you sent, a sweetsmelling fragrance, an acceptable offering, well-pleasing to Aluahim.
And my Aluahim shall fill all your need according to His riches in esteem by Mashiach OSwhy.
And to our Aluahim and Father be
